name: Jambavan
description: Jambavan, also known as Jambavat, is a prominent figure in Hindu mythology, particularly revered for his wisdom, strength, and unwavering devotion. Born during the Satya Yuga, he is often depicted as a bear-like being, though his true form is believed to be far more complex, reflecting his divine origins. Jambavan played a crucial role in the Ramayana, where he served as a wise counselor and strategist to Sugriva and Hanuman in their quest to aid Rama in rescuing Sita from Ravana. He motivated Hanuman to realize his immense potential and leap across the ocean to Lanka. Jambavan's wisdom was instrumental in the success of Rama's army. Beyond the Ramayana, Jambavan is also associated with Krishna in the Mahabharata. He engaged in a fierce battle with Krishna over the Syamantaka jewel, unaware of Krishna's true identity. After a prolonged fight, Jambavan recognized Krishna as an avatar of Vishnu and surrendered the jewel, also offering his daughter Jambavati in marriage. Jambavan represents unwavering loyalty, profound wisdom, and the ability to recognize and respect divine power. His contributions to both the Ramayana and Mahabharata solidify his status as a significant and revered figure in Hindu tradition, embodying strength, humility, and devotion. He is a symbol of the ancient, wise elder who guides and supports the hero, reminding us of the importance of recognizing our own potential and respecting divine will. He is also known for his immense strength and his ability to heal wounds and revive the fallen, making him a valuable asset in any battle. His presence often brings a sense of calm and assurance to those around him.
subtitle: Wise Bear, Loyal Counselor, Powerful Ally
achievements: Motivated Hanuman to leap to Lanka
Served as a key strategist in Rama's army
Fought valiantly in the battle against Ravana
Offered his daughter Jambavati to Krishna

keyMyths.title: Motivating Hanuman
keyMyths.description: Jambavan reminded Hanuman of his hidden powers, enabling him to cross the ocean to Lanka and find Sita. This act was crucial to Rama's mission. He said 'You are the one who can do this!'

keyMyths.title: The Syamantaka Jewel
keyMyths.description: Jambavan fought Krishna over the Syamantaka jewel, eventually recognizing Krishna as Vishnu. He surrendered the jewel and offered his daughter in marriage. The fight lasted for 28 days.

keyMyths.title: Counsel to Sugriva
keyMyths.description: Jambavan played a vital role in advising Sugriva and guiding him to form an alliance with Rama. This alliance was essential for the war against Ravana. He was a wise and trusted advisor.

sources.author: Valmiki
sources.description: The epic poem detailing Rama's life and adventures, including Jambavan's role in aiding Rama.

sources.author: Vyasa
